bank: This constant mental juggling can lead to increased stress and reduced overall efficiency.
The brain is not wired to process multiple streams of information at the same time with equal effectiveness.
Instead, we should embrace monotasking as a more sustainable and productive approach.
Many people believe they can train themselves to become better multitaskers through practice.
For instance, a simple interruption like checking a text message can take up to 20 minutes to regain full focus.
Technology companies have designed their products to exploit our tendency to multitask.
This approach not only improves the quality of output but also enhances long-term memory retention.
Many people fear that saying no will damage their relationships.
By setting clear boundaries, you actually earn respect from others.
This does not mean you should never help others, but rather be selective.
Practicing this skill in low-stakes situations can build your confidence.
However, saying no does not have to be rude or abrupt.
Without this skill, you may find yourself overwhelmed and unhappy.
Remember that every time you say yes to something, you say no to something else.
